2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.72 6.15 2.96 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.64 1.08 0.40 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.75 1.36 1.10 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.33 3.71 1.46 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 2.39 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.03 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
4.46
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.66
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.80
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

24.5%
annual
RN turnover
20.0%
annual
Administrators departed

last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
2.18
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.58
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-05-23 15 13 2 84 1 84
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-06-13 37 13 24 196 1 196
Total weighted health score
112.0
lower is better
Infection-control citations
0
last cycle

Deficiencies (67)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

67 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2026-01-30 2026-02-13
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2026-01-30 2026-02-13
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-05-23 2025-06-15
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-05-23 2025-06-15
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-05-23 2025-06-15
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2025-05-23 2025-06-15
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2025-05-23 2025-06-15
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-05-23 2025-06-15
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. E 2025-05-23 2025-06-15
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2025-05-23 2025-06-15
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. E 2025-05-23 2025-06-15
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-05-23 2025-06-15
0805 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. E 2025-05-23 2025-06-15
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-05-23 2025-06-15
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-05-23 2025-06-15
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-02-10 2025-03-07
0661 Ensure necessary information is communicated to the resident, and receiving health care provider at the time of a planned discharge. D 2025-02-10 2025-03-07
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-02-10 2025-03-07
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2025-02-10 2025-03-07
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-10-16 2024-11-07
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-09-25 2024-09-26
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-09-25 2024-09-26
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-09-09 2024-09-24
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-09-09 2024-09-24
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. E 2024-07-11 2024-07-31
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-06-13 2024-07-08
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. E 2024-06-13 2024-07-08
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. E 2024-06-13 2024-07-08
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2024-06-13 2024-07-08
